description: A flat-illustration public-safety infographic on a solid bright-blue background (horizontal 1200x600 format). On the left, inside a pale circular vignette, a cartoon man with dark hair and a blue shirt carries a Christmas tree out through an open doorway; beside him a small table holds a tear-off wall calendar showing 'DEC 30' with a handwritten red note 'take out tree!' On the right, large white bold text reads 'Get rid of your Christmas tree after Christmas or when it is dry.' followed by lighter white text 'A dry tree is dangerous. It can catch on fire easily.' Bottom-center shows the DHS seal with the 'FEMA' wordmark and the 'U.S. Fire Administration' eagle logo; bottom-right is the 'Fire is Everyone's Fight' logo. This is a seasonal fire-safety PSA (FEMA/U.S. Fire Administration), not immigration content. Friendly flat-vector holiday illustration style. Target audience: general public, post-holiday fire prevention.
notable_text: Get rid of your Christmas tree after Christmas or when it is dry. A dry tree is dangerous. It can catch on fire easily.
